sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Approving plans and specifications for the Sacramento Street Pedestrian Safety
Project (“Project”), Specification No. 24-11643-C;
2. Accepting the bid of Terocons Inc., the lowest responsive and responsible bidder;
and
3. Authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract with Terocons Inc. and any
amendments, extensions, and/or change orders until completion of the Project in
accordance with the approved plans and specifications, in an amount not to
exceed $973,638, which includes a 15% contingency for unforeseen
circumstances.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The construction budget for the Sacramento Street Pedestrian Safety Project is
$880,000, which includes a 10% contingency. Funding is available in the FY2025
budget from the funding sources indicated below:
Construction Funding for Sacramento Street Pedestrian Safety Project
State Grants (Fund 306 – Highway Safety Improvement Program)…….............$250,000
Alameda County Measure BB (Fund 135 – Bike and Pedestrian)..…………….. $239,689
Alameda County Measure B (Fund 130 – Local Streets & Roads).…………… $483,949
Total Project Funding …………………………………………………………........$973,638
Low Bid by Terocons Inc. $846,642
15% Contingency $126,996
Total Not-To-Exceed Construction Cost $973,638
sections.current_situation:
The primary purpose of the Sacramento Street Pedestrian Safety Project is to improve
safety for pedestrians and bicyclists crossing Sacramento Street in south Berkeley, in
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 9
Contract: Terocons Inc. CONSENT CALENDAR
for Sacramento Street Pedestrian Safety Project December 10, 2024
accordance with the City’s Vision Zero Action Plan which establishes a strategy to
eliminate all traffic fatalities and severe injuries by 2028 while increasing safe, healthy,
and equitable mobility for all. The Sacramento Street Pedestrian Safety Project is
intended to reduce collisions caused by driver violation of the pedestrian right of way by
making pedestrians more visible as they cross the street and reducing the speed of
turning vehicles at crosswalks. Sacramento Street is identified as a high-injury street in
the Vision Zero Action Plan and 2020 Pedestrian Plan.
The project parameters include the Sacramento Street corridor from Fairview Street to
Oregon Street. The Project will construct the following elements on Sacramento Street:
 Median modifications, including pedestrian refuge islands at Oregon Street, Julia
Street, Prince Street, and Fairview Street;
 Curb ramps at Oregon Street;
 Solar-powered Rectangular Rapid Flashing Beacon (RRFB) installation, including
pedestrian push buttons near curb ramps and bicyclist push buttons near curbs,
for crossing Sacramento Street at Oregon Street, Prince Street, and Fairview
Street; and
 Slurry seal and new pavement striping along approximately ½-mile of
Sacramento Street, from Fairview Street to Oregon Street.
On October 3, 2024 seven bids were received and opened for construction of the
Project, in amounts ranging from $846,642 to $1,539,426. The determination of the low
bidder was based on the total price of all bid items, as described in the bid documents.
sections.background:
On July 28, 2020, City Council adopted resolution no. 69,525-N.S. authorizing the City
Manager to submit a grant application to the California Highway Safety Improvement
Program (HSIP) for the amount of up to $250,000, accept the grants awarded, and
execute any resultant agreements and amendments. On December 1, 2020 City
Page 2
Page 3 of 9
Contract: Terocons Inc. CONSENT CALENDAR
for Sacramento Street Pedestrian Safety Project December 10, 2024
Council adopted resolution no. 69,634-N.S., which updated resolution no. 69,525-N.S
by identifying local matching funds.
An agreement between the City and Caltrans was executed on August 12, 2021 under
contract no 42200002, providing administrative provisions for future Caltrans grants
using State funds. $250,000 in HSIP construction funds were allocated to the City on
December 21, 2023. A Program Supplemental Agreement (PSA) was executed on May
6, 2024 as contract no. 42200002-1 formalizing this funding.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The purpose of the Project is to provide safer crossings for pedestrians and bicyclists on
Sacramento Street, which will encourage these sustainable modes of transportation.
Increasing walking and biking would help the City comply with the 2009 Berkeley
Climate Action Plan Policy 5.a that calls for expanding and improving Berkeley’s
pedestrian and bicycle infrastructure. The Climate Action Plan further states that
transportation modes such as public transit, walking, and bicycling must become the
primary means of fulfilling the City’s mobility needs in order to meet these targets.
sections.rationale:
The proposed Project components were designed to address pedestrian and bicycle
crossing safety needs along Sacramento Street, in accordance with the 2020
Pedestrian Plan and the Vision Zero Action Plan. Furthermore, the Vision Zero Action
Plan classifies Sacramento Street as a High-Injury Street in an Equity Priority Area. An
Equity Priority Area considers historic data in prioritizing infrastructure projects that
remedy systemic inequality. As a whole, the work of this Project is a significant step
forward to improve safety at intersection crossings and for people of all ages and
abilities to traverse the Sacramento Street corridor more safely by walking or biking.
Approval of the plans and specifications, accepting the bid of Terocons Inc. as the
lowest responsive and responsible bidder, and authorizing the City Manager to execute
a contract with Terocons Inc. will keep the Project on schedule in accordance with the
grant funding agreement.
sections.alternatives:
Council could opt not to take the proposed actions at this time and instead defer this
item to a future Council agenda. Such a decision would delay the construction of the
Project. Delays to the onset of construction could jeopardize successful project delivery
as the City currently has the funding to complete the work and a design team on board
that can provide continuity through the construction process. Delayed construction may
also contribute to increased construction costs due to inflation and the need to revisit
design documents to address unforeseen future construction activity or design policy
changes.
CONTACT PERSON
Wahid Amiri, Deputy Director of Public Works, Public Works, 510-981-6396
Kenneth Jung, Supervising Civil Engineer, Public Works, 510-981-7028
Jesse Peoples, Associate Civil Engineer, Public Works, 510-981-6449

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: TEROCONS INC. FOR SACRAMENTO STREET PEDESTRIAN SAFETY
PROJECT
WHEREAS, the design of the Sacramento Street Pedestrian Safety Project (Specification
No. 24-11643-C) consists of pedestrian and bicycle safety improvements across
Sacramento Street at Oregon Street, Julia Street, Tyler Street, Prince Street, Woolsey
Street, and Fairview Street; and
WHEREAS, the Project is identified as a priority project in the City’s 2020 Pedestrian
Plan; and
WHEREAS, funds are available in FY 2025 budget in the Capital Grants – State Fund
(Fund 306), the Alameda County Measure BB Bike and Pedestrian Fund (Fund 135), and
the Alameda County Measure B Local Streets and Roads Fund (Fund 130); and
WHEREAS, no other funding is required, and no other project will be delayed due to this
expenditure; and
WHEREAS, City Council adopted resolutions no. 69,525-N.S. and 69,634-N.S. which
authorized the City Manager to submit a grant application to the California Highway Safety
Improvement Program (HSIP) for the amount of up to $250,000, accept the grants
awarded, and execute any resultant agreements and amendments; and
WHEREAS, the City applied for and received HSIP grant funding in the amount of
$250,000 for construction of improvements on Sacramento Street; and
WHEREAS, the HSIP construction funds were allocated to the City on December 21,
2023; and
WHEREAS, on August 30, 2024 the City released an Invitation for Bids for the work of
this Project; and
WHEREAS, Terocons Inc. was found to be the lowest responsive and responsible bidder;
and
WHEREAS, the City has neither the staff nor the equipment needed to undertake
construction of this Project.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Plans and Specifications for the Sacramento Pedestrian Safety Project (Specification No.
24-11643-C) are approved.

December 10, 2024
